description.purpose: patient_non_compliance — 59-year-old male with I've been having confusion or difficulty concentrating for a few weeks
description.clinical_scenario: Patient presents with I've been having confusion or difficulty concentrating for a few weeks. Requires history taking, examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning.
description.focus: Addressing patient concerns and building treatment adherence

user_scenario.persona: 59-year-old male patient, 高中/中专 education, works as a retail manager. a patient who may try to hide or downplay symptoms presenting with I've been having confusion or difficulty concentrating.

patient_profile.chief_complaint: liver cirrhosis for a few weeks

patient_knows.general_concern: I've been having confusion or difficulty concentrating

hidden_truth: hepatic cirrhosis (won't mention unless specifically probed)
information_sharing_strategy.hidden_note: Only revealed through careful, specific questioning by the doctor
information_sharing_strategy.noise_note: Unrelated symptoms that may distract from the main issue
information_sharing_strategy.misleading_note: Symptoms that may point toward an incorrect diagnosis or overlap with other conditions. Doctor must carefully differentiate through targeted questioning and testing.

progressive_reveal.trigger: doctor asks a specific follow-up question about related symptoms

user_scenario.task_instructions: You are ChenXX, a 59-year-old male patient. Your education level is 高中/中专. You work as a retail manager. You are seeing a doctor because of liver cirrhosis. This has been going on for about a few weeks. You are reluctant to share certain symptoms, especially sensitive ones. Downplay severity when you can. Only reveal details if the doctor specifically asks and you feel comfortable. You don't want to make a big deal out of this. You have some symptoms you haven't mentioned to anyone — only reveal them if the doctor specifically asks about them.

communication_checks.criteria: Patient should understand the diagnosis and treatment plan
Uses clear, patient-friendly language appropriate to education level
Demonstrates empathy and builds patient trust
Addresses patient concerns, misconceptions, and questions

chief_complaint_detail.description: Complete chief complaint with onset, duration, severity, and aggravating/relieving factors

symptoms_completeness.description: All major symptoms of cirrhosis identified through systematic questioning

past_medical_history.description: Relevant past medical history including comorbidities

current_medications.description: Current medication list with doses and adherence

must_ask: Have you noticed any yellowing of your skin or eyes?
Has your abdomen been feeling swollen or bloated?
Have you noticed any confusion or difficulty concentrating?

drug_interaction_check.description: Must check drug interactions before prescribing

forbidden_actions: Agree with patient misconceptions without correction
Ignore abnormal lab values without explanation or follow-up plan
Diagnose without asking about symptom onset, duration, and progression
Diagnose without exploring at least one differential diagnosis
Dismiss patient concerns without addressing them
Use medical jargon without explanation when patient has limited health literacy
Fail to correct patient misconceptions about treatment
Prescribe contraindicated drug without documenting rationale
Order unnecessary tests without clinical justification
Fail to consider Budd-Chiari Syndrome as alternative diagnosis
Diagnose cirrhosis without ordering any supporting lab tests
Fail to assess comorbidity impact on treatment plan
Prescribe multiple new medications simultaneously without interaction check

uncertainty_events.event: Lab results show inconsistent or borderline findings
uncertainty_events.example: normal liver enzymes with significant liver disease
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Order repeat/confirmatory test, consider pre-analytical error, correlate with clinical symptoms before concluding

uncertainty_events.event: Patient reports acute worsening of symptoms during the visit
uncertainty_events.example: Patient becomes visibly more uncomfortable or reports new severe symptom
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Reassess urgency, consider acute complication, adjust pace and priorities of the consultation
